mei.relationship: Same mansion, same frequency — candidate and company are in sync with no extra energy drain
mei.daily_feel: Candidate integrates quickly, virtually no adaptation period needed
mei.energy_flow_desc: Bidirectional and balanced — neither party drains the other
mei.long_term_risk: Too comfortable may lead to stagnation; candidate must self-drive growth
mei.growth: Low. The environment won't actively push the candidate to grow
mei.stability: High. Retention is stable; bad years don't add extra drain
mei.bad_year_impact: Bad years lower personal fortune but the company adds no extra drain — minimal impact
mei.fit_for: Self-disciplined candidates who don't need external stimulation to stay driven

hr.relationship: Candidate and company are in sync — placement friction is extremely low
hr.daily_feel: Candidate integrates fast; team acceptance is high
hr.bad_year_impact: Retention risk is low; bad years won't trigger departure thoughts
hr.fit_for: Suited for steady-state operational roles. Note to company: candidate won't bring disruptive change — this is a feature, not a bug
hr.placement_risk: Low. Easy to close and stable retention, but manage expectations about 'bringing fresh perspectives'
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate can integrate seamlessly with near-zero adaptation cost

gyo.relationship: Karmic pull — candidate naturally invests in driving the company's mission
gyo.daily_feel: Candidate has an inexplicable sense of duty, proactively investing effort beyond job scope
gyo.energy_flow_desc: Candidate outputs slightly but karmic support prevents excessive fatigue
gyo.long_term_risk: Candidate may over-invest and lose sight of personal career planning
gyo.growth: Medium. Grows through mission fulfillment, but direction may deviate from personal goals
gyo.stability: Medium-high. Sense of mission provides foundational stability
gyo.bad_year_impact: Mission feels heavier in bad years but won't collapse
gyo.fit_for: Mission-driven candidates willing to commit to long-term goals

hr.relationship: Candidate has natural alignment with company mission — engagement exceeds average
hr.daily_feel: Candidate is highly self-motivated; minimal supervision needed
hr.bad_year_impact: Mission burden increases but remains stable; low departure risk
hr.fit_for: Suited for mission-oriented positions. Note to company: candidate may neglect personal development needs — proactively check in
hr.placement_risk: Low. High candidate engagement, but headhunter must ensure salary isn't suppressed by mission drive
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate has natural alignment with your mission — self-driven engagement will exceed the average hire

tai.relationship: Karmic receiver — the company naturally supports and nurtures the candidate
tai.daily_feel: Candidate feels valued and developed, settles into the role quickly
tai.energy_flow_desc: Company provides slight output; candidate is on the receiving end
tai.long_term_risk: Candidate may become over-dependent on company support, reducing self-reliance
tai.growth: Medium. Passive growth following the company's rhythm
tai.stability: Medium-high. Karmic support exists but full dependence isn't sustainable
tai.bad_year_impact: Company provides a buffer; impact is smaller than facing it alone
tai.fit_for: Candidates who accept guidance and develop steadily within mature systems

hr.relationship: Company naturally provides support and resources to the candidate
hr.daily_feel: Short adaptation period; candidate gets up to speed quickly
hr.bad_year_impact: Company buffer keeps departure risk below average
hr.fit_for: Suited for positions with training programs. Note to company: candidate needs guidance — hands-off approach reduces effectiveness
hr.placement_risk: Low. Natural interaction, but verify the company actually has training resources
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate will grow rapidly under your development framework — high return on training investment

ei.relationship: Candidate and company invest in each other, forming a positive cycle
ei.daily_feel: Giving pays off — positive cycle keeps candidate satisfaction high
ei.energy_flow_desc: Candidate invests proactively; company's reciprocation prevents fatigue
ei.long_term_risk: If the positive cycle breaks (e.g., manager change), candidate may suddenly feel empty
ei.growth: High. Bidirectional elevation — one of the best growth environments
ei.stability: Medium-high. Stable while the cycle continues; fades quickly if broken
ei.bad_year_impact: Cycle may weaken in bad years but won't reverse into drain
ei.fit_for: Candidates willing to give first and believe in mutual benefit

hr.relationship: Candidate and company form a positive mutual-benefit cycle
hr.daily_feel: Both sides elevate each other; candidate satisfaction is high
hr.bad_year_impact: Cycle may weaken but won't reverse — low retention risk
hr.fit_for: The most ideal long-term placement. Note to company: maintaining feedback mechanisms is key to retaining this candidate
hr.placement_risk: Very low. Most stable recommendation; lowest departure probability during guarantee period
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate and your company have strong mutual-benefit potential — both sides will improve over time

shin.relationship: Company invests resources to develop the candidate; candidate is the beneficiary
shin.daily_feel: Candidate feels valued and mentored; loyalty builds naturally
shin.energy_flow_desc: Company actively invests; candidate receives development
shin.long_term_risk: Over-reliance on mentoring may limit independence after leaving
shin.growth: High. Mentored growth is fast; internalization is key
shin.stability: High. Shin position is a stable beneficiary role; strong retention intent
shin.bad_year_impact: Company investment may decrease but won't reverse into drain
shin.fit_for: Strong learners who quickly internalize others' experience

hr.relationship: Company is willing to develop the candidate; candidate's receptivity is high
hr.daily_feel: Candidate feels valued; loyalty exceeds average
hr.bad_year_impact: Development effect may weaken but won't reverse; retention is stable
hr.fit_for: Suited for positions with development budget and intent. Candidate will convert training into output
hr.placement_risk: Low. High candidate loyalty; very low risk during guarantee period
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate will convert your training investment into tangible output — high ROI on development

yu.relationship: Candidate is the giver — proactively manages team relationships while company enjoys the atmosphere
yu.daily_feel: Candidate proactively invests social energy; fatigue is hard to notice from outside
yu.energy_flow_desc: Candidate continuously outputs; company receives. Candidate's exhaustion goes undetected
yu.long_term_risk: Social-layer comfort doesn't equal deep satisfaction — candidate may appear happy while internally burning out
yu.growth: Medium. Social skills grow but professional depth requires self-effort
yu.stability: Medium. Candidate's sense of over-giving accumulates; periodic recharging needed
yu.bad_year_impact: Social energy drops in bad years; giving becomes harder
yu.fit_for: Socially strong candidates who enjoy building relationships

hr.relationship: Candidate proactively manages team atmosphere, but drain is hidden
hr.daily_feel: Candidate energizes the team, but fatigue isn't visible
hr.bad_year_impact: Social energy drops in bad years — watch for burnout signals
hr.fit_for: Suited for positions needing team cohesion. Note to company: candidate's contributions need to be seen and reciprocated
hr.placement_risk: Medium. Short-term stable, but watch for burnout risk after one year
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate will naturally elevate team atmosphere, but regular positive feedback is recommended

sui.relationship: Candidate is the sui party — company provides resources but candidate is continuously drained
sui.daily_feel: Initially feels well-supported by the company; fatigue accumulates after six months
sui.energy_flow_desc: Company invests heavily; candidate's returns diminish. Year one is the best
sui.long_term_risk: Drained to the point of wanting to leave but unable to — trapped in a burnout cycle
sui.growth: High but passive. Company pushes growth; candidate has no say in direction
sui.stability: Medium-low. Comfort decreases over time; fatigue accumulates
sui.bad_year_impact: Bad year + sui party = double drain; departure risk spikes
sui.fit_for: High stress-tolerance candidates with a clear exit timeline

hr.relationship: Company invests resources but candidate's capacity is limited; long-term drain risk is high
hr.daily_feel: Candidate performs well initially but sustainability is limited
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year + sui party = high departure risk
hr.fit_for: Suited for positions with clear phase goals. Be honest with company: long-term retention rate is below average
hr.placement_risk: Medium-high. May be fine during guarantee period, but one-year departure rate is elevated
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate will perform strongly initially — suggest setting milestone goals to maintain momentum

an.relationship: Candidate leads stability, continuously outputting energy to hold things together
an.daily_feel: Like holding an umbrella — candidate stabilizes the team but drain goes unnoticed
an.energy_flow_desc: Candidate continuously outputs to maintain stability; company gets security from it
an.long_term_risk: An party's continuous output goes unrecognized until burnout hits
an.growth: Medium. Management skills grow but candidate gets locked into the stabilizer role
an.stability: Surface high, actual drain. Stable while candidate is there; collapses if they can't hold on
an.bad_year_impact: Stabilization pressure doubles in bad years — could be the last straw
an.fit_for: Strong managers who set boundaries. Without boundaries, they'll be hollowed out

hr.relationship: Candidate holds things steady but long-term umbrella-style output risks burnout
hr.daily_feel: Candidate stabilizes the team, but their drain is hidden
hr.bad_year_impact: Stabilization pressure doubles in bad years; departure or breakdown risk rises
hr.fit_for: Suited for teams needing a steady hand. Note to company: distribute the pressure — don't let one person carry all the stability
hr.placement_risk: Medium. Short-term stable, but watch for burnout risk after two years
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate will stabilize your team, but avoid concentrating all stability responsibility on one person

kai.relationship: Company continuously extracts energy from the candidate — 'nothing else is bearable'
kai.daily_feel: Subtle at first; after six months, nothing feels like enough
kai.energy_flow_desc: Company absorbs candidate's stability; one-way energy loss
kai.long_term_risk: Very difficult after 2-3 years; total energy depletion
kai.growth: Low. All energy is being extracted; no reserves for growth
kai.stability: Low. Kai position is the most unstable placement
kai.bad_year_impact: Bad year + kai party = potentially career lowest point
kai.fit_for: Only viable with strong external support (mentor, side income); otherwise not recommended long-term

hr.relationship: Company continuously extracts energy from the candidate
hr.daily_feel: Candidate starts feeling inadequate after six months; output declines
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year + kai party = departure is almost certain
hr.fit_for: Only suitable for short-term projects or consultant roles. Headhunter must disclose long-term retention risk
hr.placement_risk: High. Departure intent may emerge within the guarantee period — evaluate carefully
hr.pitch_to_company: Candidate is suited for short-term projects; suggest consultant-style engagement to reduce risk for both sides

ki.relationship: Company applies pressure that drives candidate growth — pressure-based interaction
ki.daily_feel: Short-term challenge and achievement; long-term fatigue accumulation
ki.energy_flow_desc: Company applies pressure; candidate absorbs. Drain isn't low but growth returns exist
ki.long_term_risk: Pressure has an upper limit — beyond it, growth turns to harm
ki.growth: High but with cost. Pressure-driven growth needs rest periods
ki.stability: Medium. Stable pressure is good stimulation; excessive pressure causes collapse
ki.bad_year_impact: Bad year pressure may crush rather than push
ki.fit_for: High stress-tolerance candidates who thrive on challenge and can self-regulate

hr.relationship: Company's high-pressure environment drives candidate growth
hr.daily_feel: Candidate is active short-term but long-term fatigue needs monitoring
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year pressure may overwhelm the candidate
hr.fit_for: Suited for high-pressure, high-reward positions. Note to company: pair with rest mechanisms — don't just apply pressure
hr.placement_risk: Medium. How long candidate lasts depends on the company's pressure management
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate performs well under pressure, but we recommend pairing with reasonable rest mechanisms

sei.relationship: Candidate grows through friction with the company; becomes stronger after adjustment
sei.daily_feel: Uncomfortable initially; after adaptation, realizes they've become stronger
sei.energy_flow_desc: Company's traits refine the candidate; passive strengthening with low drain
sei.long_term_risk: Initial adjustment period requires patience; once past, it's stable
sei.growth: High. Post-friction growth is solid and lasting
sei.stability: High. Sei position is one of the most stable after adjustment
sei.bad_year_impact: Friction feeling intensifies in bad years but won't collapse
sei.fit_for: Candidates who tolerate initial discomfort for long-term payoff

hr.relationship: Candidate grows through adjustment; performance stabilizes after adaptation
hr.daily_feel: Candidate needs initial adaptation but shows high retention intent afterward
hr.bad_year_impact: Friction feeling increases but remains stable; no impact on retention
hr.fit_for: Suited for positions requiring long-term development. Note to company: don't rush to judge in the first three months
hr.placement_risk: Low. Very stable once past the adjustment period; high long-term retention rate
hr.pitch_to_company: This candidate needs a three-month adjustment period, but afterward they'll be your most stable long-term asset
